name: Matteo Correggia (Azienda Agricola)
blurb: The late Matteo Correggia was the producer who persuaded wine critics in the 1990s that Roero Rosso deserved to be taken as seriously as Barolo, and the estate his wife Ornella runs with the same convictions is still the appellation's reference point. Because it sits fifteen minutes from Alba and is not heavily promoted on the touring circuit, it tends to receive serious wine travelers rather than bus groups. The organic vineyard practices are genuine rather than marketing copy; Slow Food Italy has covered the estate as a model for the region.
